title: GreenGateway Tool Definitions
description: Draft 2020-12 JSON Schema for provider-agnostic MCP/API tool definitions. This file describes what tools are and how their calls map to upstream HTTP requests; runtime policy remains in the separate RBAC policy document.

tools.description: Tool definitions. Tool names must be unique; that cross-entry check is enforced by the Rust loader.

name.description: Stable tool name used by callers and policy. Names are unique within one tools file.

description.description: Human-readable tool description exposed to future MCP callers.

input_json_schema.description: A complete JSON Schema document for this tool's argument object. The loader also compiles this schema to reject malformed schemas at load time.

json_schema_document.description: A JSON Schema document for a tool argument object. Tool schemas must declare an object-valued properties keyword so path-template placeholders can be checked against explicit arguments. Semantic schema validity is enforced by jsonschema::validator_for at registry load time.

properties.description: Named tool-call arguments accepted by this tool.

method.description: HTTP method used for the future upstream request executor. The Rust loader rejects methods outside the known HTTP method set.

path_template.description: Absolute upstream path template using {param}-style placeholders, such as /v1/widgets/{widget_id}.

query_name.description: Outbound query parameter name to write.

required.description: Whether the future executor should require this argument before constructing the request.

mode.description: Construct the future outbound request body by serializing the entire tool arguments object as JSON.
